title: phlox gladiformis f. gooddingii
intro: Phlox gladiformis f. gooddingii, also known as Goodding's phlox, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the phlox genus and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The Goodding's phlox is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and its leaves can also be used in herbal remedies, such as salves and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
